source_jobs.title: Senior Manufacturing Engineer (Assembly)
source_jobs.description:
About the Role

At H3X https://www.h3x.tech/, we are building the most advanced electric motors in the world for sustainable aviation, marine, industrial, and defense applications. At 12 kW/kg continuous, the HPDM family of integrated motor drives is a step change in electric propulsion technology and removes one of the main barriers blocking widespread proliferation of electric and hybrid aircraft. Our motors range in size from 30 kW to 30 MW, include integrated drives, and are 3X lighter than competitors’ systems.

H3X is looking to hire a Senior Manufacturing Engineer to support production lines across all the HPDM products. The production team is rapidly deploying new processes and improvements to meet the growing demand for the HPDM products. A high degree of self-motivation and responsibility is required since this role will be given significant freedom and will be directly responsible for the production of a multitude of parts.



Responsibilities

 - Scale mechanical manufacturing processes to meet growing company demand

 - Manage new product introduction (NPI) for sub-system and full system assemblies

 - Design and implement manufacturing tools and fixtures using SolidWorks

 - Troubleshoot and resolve assembly manufacturing issues

 - Own the non-conformance and corrective action process for respective production area

 - Identify and implement continuous improvement opportunities for yield, performance, and cost.

 - Create and maintain production work instructions and inspection plans.

 - Collaborate across departments to streamline processes and meet project timelines

 - Support design engineering with design for manufacturability (DFM) and prototype procurement.



Required Qualifications

 - Bachelor's or Graduate Degree in Mechanical Engineering or similar

 - 5+ years of experience as a Manufacturing Engineer

 - 5+ years of CAD software experience (SolidWorks preferred)

 - Experience scaling complex assemblies to higher production volume

 - Expert understanding of mechanical drawings

 - Superior knowledge of GD&T and part inspection

 - Excellent written and Verbal communication skills

 - Organized and strong attention to detail.

 - Exceptional understanding of engineering fundamentals

 - Vast knowledge of manufacturing processes (machining, assembly, composites, etc)

 - Action biased, results-driven mentality

 - Willingness to learn quickly

 - A high degree of emotional intelligence: ability to collaborate closely with coworkers in a respectful and empathetic manner

 - Fundamentally curious and motivated by first principals



Preferred Qualifications

 - Experience designing manufacturing floor layouts

 - Understanding of electric motors or high voltage systems

 - Experience working in a startup environment

 - Experience with ISO9001 and/or AS9100 standards



Benefits & Compensation

 - Compensation: $140,000 - $200,000 annual salary

 - Employee equity incentive plan

 - Health insurance: medical, vision, dental, ST & LT disability, and life

 - Gym membership stipend (up to $60/month)

 - Outdoor Recreation Reimbursement ($900 annual maximum)

 - Flexible hours (deliverable-based goals)

 - Flexible time off (just need manager approval)

 - Relocation package

 - Monthly company events

 - 401k program



The Company

H3X Technologies is a U.S.-based manufacturer of electric motors for sustainable aviation, marine, industrial, and defense applications. They have developed a family of integrated motor drives that can scale in power from 30kW to 30MW with power densities as high as 12 kW/kg and best-in-class efficiency. This high performance is enabled by novel scalable core technology and proprietary manufacturing processes for motor stacking, winding, and assembly. The design, manufacturing, and testing of their products is done in-house at their headquarters in Louisville, Colorado.

H3X’s world-class team is composed of experts from Tesla, SpaceX, GE Aviation, and Siemens with deep kno

source_jobs.title: Manufacturing Engineer (Stator)
source_jobs.description:
JOB PURPOSE:
At H3X, we are building the most advanced electric motors in the world for sustainable aviation, marine, industrial, and defense applications. At 12 kW/kg continuous, the HPDM family of integrated motor drives is a step change in electric propulsion technology and removes one of the main barriers blocking widespread proliferation of electric and hybrid aircraft. Our motors range in size from 30 kW to 30 MW and are 3X lighter than competitors’ systems.

About the Role:

H3X is looking to hire a Stator Manufacturing Engineer to support stator assemblies across multiple product lines. The production team is rapidly deploying new processes and improvements to meet the growing demand for the HPDM products. A high degree of self-motivation and responsibility is required since this role will be given significant freedom and will be directly responsible for the production of a multitude of parts.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Responsible for stator assemblies across multiple product lines.

 - Work with design engineers to establish production requirements

 - Conceptualize new and novel approaches to solve manufacturing problems

 - Design manufacturing tools and fixtures in SolidWorks

 - Analyze recurring manufacturing discrepancies and implement solutions

 - Collaborate with design engineers on implementation of manufacturing feedback

 - Conduct PDRs and CDRs for sub-systems for which you are responsible

 - Execute first application and testing of new fixtures and process improvements

 - Interface with production operations team and assembly technicians

 - Create and implement production travelers

 - Commission and scale manufacturing processes to meet production rate requirements

 - Identify workplace hazards and implement safe processes

 - Maintain testing and production equipment when necessary

 - Source hardware for manufacturing systems as needed

 - Collaborate with other manufacturing and design engineers to identify, improve, and expand in-house manufacturing capabilities.



ADDITIONAL RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Identify workplace hazards and implement safe processes

 - Maintain testing and production equipment when necessary

 - Source hardware for manufacturing systems as needed

 - Collaborate with other manufacturing and design engineers to identify, improve, and expand in-house manufacturing capabilities.



REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS:

 - Bachelor’s or Graduate Degree in Mechanical Engineering or similar.

 - 4+ years of experience as a Manufacturing Engineer.

 - Hands-on experience assembling mechanical systems.

 - 5+ years of CAD software experience (SolidWorks preferred).

 - Prior work with high voltage insulation systems and motor manufacturing.

 - Thorough understanding of mechanical drawings.

 - Superior knowledge of GD&T and part inspection.

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

 - Organized and strong attention to detail.

 - Exceptional understanding of engineering fundamentals.

 - Vast knowledge of manufacturing processes (machining, assembly, composites, etc).

 - Action biased, results-driven mentality.

 - Willingness to learn quickly.

 - A high degree of emotional intelligence: ability to collaborate closely with coworkers in a respectful and empathetic manner.

 - Fundamentally curious and motivated by first principles Position requires access to CUI/ITAR/EAR controlled information.



PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS:

 - Experience manufacturing electric motors, inverters, or other mechatronic assemblies

 - Experience around PCBA and wiring harness fabrication and sourcing

 - Experience developing production automation systems

 - Experience working in a startup environment
   
   
   
   COMPANY BENEFITS:

 - Employee equity incentive plan

 - Health insurance: medical, vision, dental, ST & LT disability, and life

 - Gym membership stipend (up to $60/month)

 - Outdoor Recreation Reimbursement ($900 annual maximum)

 - Flexible hours (deliverable-based goals)

source_jobs.title: Mechanical Design Engineer Intern
source_jobs.description:
About the Role

At H3X https://www.h3x.tech/, we are building the most advanced electric motors in the world for sustainable aviation, marine, industrial, and defense applications. At 12 kW/kg continuous, the HPDM family of integrated motor drives is a step change in electric propulsion technology and removes one of the main barriers blocking widespread proliferation of electric and hybrid aircraft. Our motors range in size from 30 kW to 30 MW, include integrated drives, and are 3X lighter than competitors’ systems.

H3X is looking to hire a mechanical design engineering intern this Fall to support the development across all aspects of the HPDM product line. This is not an ordinary internship- you will be given the full responsibility of a mechanical design engineer. The engineering team is intentionally small and operates at a very fast pace. A high degree of self-motivation and responsibility is required as you will be given significant freedom and will be directly responsible for your parts. We are looking to hire a full time intern with a focus on the Fall 2026 Semester for a minimum of 12 weeks, but open to extended periods as well based on candidate availability.



Responsibilities

 - Requirements/Concept
   
   - Establish and document requirements
   
   - Conceptualize designs and product architectures that meet program requirements
   
   - Present in Concept Design Reviews

 - Design and Analysis
   
   - Design parts and assemblies utilizing SolidWorks
   
   - Perform and document mechanical analyses, including hand calculations, FEA, CFD, etc. for structural, modal, thermal, and/or fluid aspects of products
   
   - Present in Preliminary and Critical Design Reviews
   
   - Generate engineering drawings per ASME and GD&T standards, perform tolerance stack-ups

 - Prototype and Manufacturing
   
   - Compile complete Bills of Materials
   
   - Provide technical supply chain support for sourcing hardware
   
   - Work with technicians and manufacturing engineers to build initial prototypes

 - Testing and Validation
   
   - Create a Design Validation Plan and Report (DVP&R) for new products and/or subsystems
   
   - Support testing execution and characterization of new designs
   
   - Interpret test results, identify root-cause of issues, and make design recommendations to meet the highest level of reliability and performance

 - Integration
   
   - Integrate sub-systems into full systems
   
   - Support future production builds or complete system tests

 - Other
   
   - Identify workplace hazards and implement safe processes
   
   - Maintain testing and production equipment where necessary



Required Qualifications

 - Pursuing a Bachelor’s or Graduate Degree in Mechanical Engineering or similar

 - GPA of 3.0 or higher

 - Hands-on experience assembling mechanical systems

 - SolidWorks proficient (or similar)

 - Excellent written and verbal communication

 - Exceptional understanding of fundamentals

 - Action biased, results-driven mentality

 - Willingness to learn quickly



Preferred Qualifications

 - Experience with manufacturing processes (machining, Metal AM, & composites)

 - Experience with producing 2D part drawings & GD&T

 - Experience with linear structural FEA



Benefits & Compensation

 - $23.00 - $37.00 / hr based on school level & experience

 - Relocation stipend

 - Monthly company events

 - Friday Team Lunch



The Company

H3X Technologies is a U.S.-based manufacturer of electric motors for sustainable aviation, marine, industrial, and defense applications. They have developed a family of integrated motor drives that can scale in power from 30kW to 30MW with power densities as high as 12 kW/kg and best-in-class efficiency. This high performance is enabled by novel scalable core technology and proprietary manufacturing processes for motor stacking, winding, and assembly. The design, manufacturing, and testing of their products is done in-house at their headquarters in Louisvil

source_jobs.title: Assembly Technician
source_jobs.description:
About the Role

At H3X https://www.h3x.tech/, we are building the most advanced electric motors in the world for sustainable aviation, marine, industrial, and defense applications. At 12 kW/kg continuous, the HPDM family of integrated motor drives is a step change in electric propulsion technology and removes one of the main barriers blocking widespread proliferation of electric and hybrid aircraft. Our motors range in size from 30 kW to 30 MW, include integrated drives, and are 3X lighter than competitors’ systems.

We are looking to hire an Assembly Technician who will assist in the manufacturing of prototype electric drive systems and facilitate testing. You should be experienced with standard mechanical system assembly and troubleshooting hardware. You will be a key part of the core engineering team and will have a direct impact on the trajectory of the company.



Responsibilities

 - Assemble production motor drive systems and sub-systems.

 - Handle, mix and work with process chemicals and hazardous materials.

 - Assist in maintenance and upkeep of production processing equipment.

 - Read, comprehend and perform technical processes as defined by internal work instructions, standards, or other specifications.

 - Coordinate with manufacturing & design engineers regarding assembly process issues and improvements.



Required Qualifications

 - High intelligence, high energy, and high integrity.

 - High School Diploma or equivalent.

 - 2+ years of hands-on mechanical system experience in the following areas:
   
   - Fastening
   
   - Epoxy application
   
   - Component fabrication
   
   - Wiring harness manufacturing

 - Proficiency in comprehending and improving manufacturing processes.

 - Able to troubleshoot assembly issues.

 - Able to conceptualize assembly processes from CAD models.

 - Excellent written and verbal communicator to effectively share knowledge and build the collective mindshare of the company.

 - Organized and obsessive attention to detail: no room for oversight when making flight critical systems.

 - Humble and open-minded: willingness to accept feedback, learn, and consistently improve upon yourself and the products for which you will be responsible.

 - A high degree of emotional intelligence: ability to collaborate closely with coworkers in a respectful and empathetic manner.

 - Fundamentally curious and motivated my first principles.

 - This position requires access to CUI/ITAR/EAR controlled information.



Preferred Qualifications

 - Experience assembling flight qualified aerospace parts.

 - Experience with the following skillsets: Brazing, Soldering, Milling, Turning, Epoxy encapsulation.

 - Experience with PCB assembly and wire harnessing.

 - Experience developing novel manufacturing processes.

 - Experience with GD&T standards (e.g. ASME Y14.5)

 - Experience with AS9100 supply chain practices.

 - Experience with tool calibration processes.

 - Associates or technical trade degree.



Compensation & Benefits

 - Pay Range: $22/hr - $34/hr

 - Employee equity incentive plan

 - Health insurance: Medical, Vision, Dental, ST & LT Disability, and Life

 - Gym membership stipend (up to $60/month)

 - Outdoor Recreation Reimbursement ($900 annual maximum)

 - Flexible hours (deliverable-based goals)

 - PTO Policy (80 hours per 2000 hours, accrued per hour, PTO can be taken by the hour)

 - Continuing professional education benefit

 - Relocation package

 - Monthly company events

 - 401k program



The Company

H3X Technologies is a U.S.-based manufacturer of electric motors for sustainable aviation, marine, industrial, and defense applications. They have developed a family of integrated motor drives that can scale in power from 30kW to 30MW with power densities as high as 12 kW/kg and best-in-class efficiency. This high performance is enabled by novel scalable core technology and proprietary manufacturing processes for motor stacking, winding, and assembly. The design, manufac

source_jobs.title: Manufacturing Engineer Intern
source_jobs.description:
About the Role

At H3X https://www.h3x.tech/, we are building the most advanced electric motors in the world for sustainable aviation, marine, industrial, and defense applications. At 12 kW/kg continuous, the HPDM family of integrated motor drives is a step change in electric propulsion technology and removes one of the main barriers blocking widespread proliferation of electric and hybrid aircraft. Our motors range in size from 30 kW to 30 MW, include integrated drives, and are 3X lighter than competitors’ systems.

H3X is looking to hire a manufacturing engineering intern to support the low volume production line across all the HPDM product lines. This is not an ordinary internship- you will be given the full responsibility of a manufacturing engineer. The production team is rapidly deploying new processes and improvements to meet the growing demand for the HPDM product lines. A high degree of self-motivation and responsibility is required as you will be given significant freedom and will be directly responsible to produce your assigned parts. We are looking to hire a full time intern with a focus on the Fall 2026 Semester for a minimum of 12 weeks, but open to extended periods as well based on candidate availability.



Responsibilities

 - REQUIREMENTS / CONCEPT
   
   - Work with design engineers to establish production requirements
   
   - Conceptualize new and novel approaches to solve manufacturing problems
   
   - Propose new materials, fixture concepts, equipment or other

 - DESIGN AND ANALYSIS
   
   - Design tools and fixtures to improve manufacturing processes
   
   - Analyze recurring manufacturing discrepancies and implement solutions
   
   - Collaborate with design engineers on implementation of manufacturing feedback

 - PRODUCTION VALIDATION AND INTEGRATION
   
   - Execute first application and testing of new fixtures and process improvements
   
   - Interface with manufacturing engineers and assembly technicians
   
   - Create, implement, and revise process plans and mBOMs to support production
   
   - Lead LEAN manufacturing processes in support of continuous improvement initiatives
   
   

Required Qualifications

 - Pursuing a Bachelor’s or Graduate Degree in Mechanical Engineering or similar

 - GPA of 3.0 or higher

 - Hands-on experience assembling mechanical systems

 - SolidWorks proficient (or similar)

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills

 - Organized and strong attention to detail

 - Exceptional understanding of engineering fundamentals

 - Action biased, results-driven mentality

 - Willingness to learn quickly



Preferred Qualifications

 - Experience with manufacturing processes (machining, assembly, & composites)

 - Experience with producing 2D part drawings and GD&T

 - Experience with inspection methods and metrology equipment



Benefits & Compensation

 - $23.00 - $37.00 / hr based on school level & experience

 - Relocation stipend

 - Monthly company events

 - Friday Team Lunch



The Company

H3X Technologies is a U.S.-based manufacturer of electric motors for sustainable aviation, marine, industrial, and defense applications. They have developed a family of integrated motor drives that can scale in power from 30kW to 30MW with power densities as high as 12 kW/kg and best-in-class efficiency. This high performance is enabled by novel scalable core technology and proprietary manufacturing processes for motor stacking, winding, and assembly. The design, manufacturing, and testing of their products is done in-house at their headquarters in Louisville, Colorado.

H3X’s world-class team is composed of experts from Tesla, SpaceX, GE Aviation, and Siemens with deep knowledge in electric machines, power electronics, material science, control systems, and advanced manufacturing. They take pride in our multidisciplinary approach, relentless dedication, high capital efficiency, and Skunkworks-style execution.

H3X has raised over $30M and has backing from top VC firm

source_jobs.title: Senior Manufacturing Engineer (Electronics)
source_jobs.description:
About the Role

At H3X https://www.h3x.tech/, we are building the most advanced electric motors in the world for sustainable aviation, marine, industrial, and defense applications. At 12 kW/kg continuous, the HPDM family of integrated motor drives is a step change in electric propulsion technology and removes one of the main barriers blocking widespread proliferation of electric and hybrid aircraft. Our motors range in size from 30 kW to 30 MW, include integrated drives, and are 3X lighter than competitors’ systems.

H3X is seeking an Electronics Manufacturing Engineer to oversee PCB assembly and wire harness manufacturing and procurement. This role involves implementing new processes and improvements to meet the increasing demand. The position requires a high degree of self-motivation and responsibility, providing significant autonomy and direct responsibility for the production of numerous components.



Responsibilities

 - Scale electronics manufacturing to meet growing company demand.

 - Manage new product introduction (NPI) for PCB and harness assemblies.

 - Troubleshoot and resolve electronics manufacturing issues.

 - Ensure quality control through documentation and corrections for non-conformances.

 - Identify and implement continuous improvement opportunities for yield, performance, and cost.

 - Develop manufacturing instructions and tests for PCB and wire harnesses.

 - Evaluate and assist contract manufacturers to ensure quality and reliability of electronics supply chain.

 - Collaborate across departments to streamline processes and meet project timelines.

 - Support design engineering with design for manufacturability (DFM) and prototype procurement.



Required Qualifications

 - Bachelor’s or graduate degree in Electrical Engineering, Electronics, Computer Engineering, Mechanical Engineering or equivalent experience.

 - 5+ years of experience as an Electronics Manufacturing or Design Engineer.

 - Strong functional knowledge of PCB fabrication and assembly processes at low to high volumes.

 - Experience with PCB fabrication and assembly procurement processes.

 - Understanding of power electronic functions and high voltage systems.

 - Proficiency in hand soldering TH and SMD components.

 - Fundamental 3D CAD and circuit board design experience.

 - Experience with quality standards (IPC-A-610, IPC-6012, etc.).

 - Experience with AOI, X-ray, and flying probe tests PCB design experience.

 - Ability to develop test methods, inspection plans, and quality control procedures.

 - Expertise in designing custom fixturing and tools associated with electronics manufacturing.

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills.

 - Organized with strong attention to detail.

 - Solid understanding of engineering fundamentals.

 - Results-driven with a willingness to learn quickly.

 - High emotional intelligence for effective collaboration.

 - Fundamental curiosity and motivation by first principles.



Preferred Qualifications

 - Programming experience in C, C++, Python, or other scripting languages.

 - Experience designing PCBA layouts and schematics.

 - Knowledge of electronic component BOM management and vendor list development.

 - Familiarity with communication protocols (i2c, SPI, CAN, Ethernet).

 - Experience with ISO9001 and/or AS9100 standards.

 - Experience working in a startup environment.

 - Knowledge of ESD controls.



Benefits & Compensation

 - Salary Target: $120,000 - $180,000

 - Employee equity incentive plan

 - Health insurance: medical, vision, dental, ST & LT disability, and life

 - Gym membership stipend (up to $60/month)

 - Outdoor Recreation Reimbursement ($900 annual maximum)

 - Flexible hours (deliverable-based goals)

 - Flexible time off (just need manager approval)

 - Relocation package

 - Monthly company events

 - 401k program



The Company

source_jobs.title: Inventory Specialist
source_jobs.description:
About the Role

At H3X https://www.h3x.tech/, we are building the most advanced electric motors in the world for sustainable aviation, marine, industrial, and defense applications. At 12 kW/kg continuous, the HPDM family of integrated motor drives is a step change in electric propulsion technology and removes one of the main barriers blocking widespread proliferation of electric and hybrid aircraft. Our motors range in size from 30 kW to 30 MW, include integrated drives, and are 3X lighter than competitors’ systems.

The Inventory Specialist is a critical operational role within H3X Technologies’ Supply Chain & Operations team. This individual will be responsible for the accurate receipt, storage, and distribution of materials that directly support production and engineering operations. Beyond day-to-day execution, this role carries a meaningful ownership stake in inventory data accuracy and is expected to actively drive continuous improvement of inventory practices over time.

The ideal candidate brings hands-on experience with inventory systems and physical material handling, takes pride in getting the details right, and approaches process gaps as problems to be solved rather than accepted. This role works closely with production planning, engineering, and quality teams and requires a high degree of reliability, organization, and professional communication.



Responsibilities

Cycle Counting & Inventory Accuracy

 - Own the full execution of structured weekly cycle counts across all assigned inventory locations.

 - Record, investigate, and reconcile physical count discrepancies against MES system data.

 - Maintain a running history of variance trends and escalate recurring or systemic discrepancies for resolution.

 - Accuracy of the inventory record is a primary responsibility of this role.

Kitting & Production Support

 - Pick, kit, and stage materials for production orders and engineering builds in accordance with production plans.

 - Ensure kits and line-side inventory are complete, accurate, and on schedule.

 - Proactively communicate material shortages or readiness risks to production planning to prevent build delays.

Inventory Operations & Material Flow

 - Receive, replenish, and fulfill raw materials, parts, and hardware to support production and shop operations.

 - Stock and organize materials in designated locations — bins, drawers, racks, and line-side areas — maintaining accurate placement and proper stock rotation (FIFO/FEFO) at all times.

 - Move and redistribute inventory to improve organization and material flow efficiency as operations evolve.

Receiving & Material Verification

 - Verify all incoming material for conformity with documentation, including part numbers, revisions, quantities, condition, and traceability requirements.

 - Execute accurate MES (Manufacturo) transactions for receipts, issues, and transfers in a timely manner.

 - Support purchasing and material planning activities, including demand signal communication and shortage identification.

MES Data Integrity

 - Administer and maintain accurate inventory data in Manufacturo, including all transactions, work order issues, receipts, and inventory adjustments.

 - Identify and resolve data discrepancies promptly and escalate systemic issues to appropriate stakeholders.

 - Support physical inventory and audit activities as required.

Continuous Improvement

 - Actively contribute to the ongoing improvement of inventory practices, including count methodology, storage layout, kitting processes, and material flow.

 - This role is expected to not only execute current processes but to identify what is not working and bring forward actionable solutions.

 - Execute special projects including layout optimization, kitting improvements, and process standardization as a regular part of the role.

 - Support organizational metrics and continuous improvement initiatives across the supply chain function.

Compliance & Safety

 - Abide by all

source_jobs.title: Senior/Staff Mechanical Design Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About the Role

At H3X https://www.h3x.tech/, we are building the most advanced electric motors in the world for sustainable aviation, marine, industrial, and defense applications. At 12 kW/kg continuous, the HPDM family of integrated motor drives is a step change in electric propulsion technology and removes one of the main barriers blocking widespread proliferation of electric and hybrid aircraft. Our motors range in size from 30 kW to 30 MW, include integrated drives, and are 3X lighter than competitors’ systems.

Mechanical design engineers are responsible for bringing new designs to life from clean sheet concept generation through early prototype testing. Mechanical Design Engineers will work with highly interdisciplinary teams to bring H3X products to life. The engineering team operates like Skunkworks - small, highly technical, and very fast. All mechanical design engineers are given significant design freedom and will be directly responsible for flight-critical parts.



Responsibilities

Requirements/Concept

 - Establish and document requirements

 - Conceptualize designs and product architectures that meet program requirements

 - Present in Concept Design Reviews

Design and Analysis

 - Design parts and assemblies utilizing SolidWorks

 - Perform and document mechanical analyses, including hand calculations, FEA, CFD, etc. for structural, modal, thermal, and/or fluid aspects of products

 - Present in Preliminary and Critical Design Reviews

 - Generate engineering drawings per ASME and GD&T standards, perform tolerance stack-ups

Prototype and Manufacturing

 - Compile complete Bills of Materials

 - Provide technical supply chain support for sourcing hardware

 - Work with technicians and manufacturing engineers to build initial prototypes

Testing and Validation

 - Create a Design Validation Plan and Report (DVP&R) for new products and/or subsystems

 - Support testing execution and characterization of new designs

 - Interpret test results, identify root-cause of issues, and make design recommendations to meet the highest level of reliability and performance

Integration

 - Integrate sub-systems into full systems

 - Support future production builds or complete system tests

Other

 - Identify workplace hazards and implement safe processes

 - Maintain testing and production equipment where necessary



Required Qualifications

 - Bachelor’s or Graduate Degree in Mechanical Engineering or similar.

 - Hands-on experience assembling mechanical systems.

 - 6+ years of CAD software experience (SolidWorks preferred).

 - 6+ years’ experience with producing 2D part drawings & GD&T.

 - Experience with static structural FEA (ANSYS preferred) (Familiarity with thermal and modal analysis preferred).

 - Excellent written and verbal communicator to effectively share knowledge and build the collective mindshare of the company.

 - Fundamentally curious and motivated by first principles.

 - Organized and obsessive attention to detail: no room for oversight when designing flight critical systems.

 - Action biased, results oriented mentality.

 - Humble and open minded: willingness to accept feedback and learn, consistently improve upon yourself and the product for which you will be responsible.

 - A high degree of emotional intelligence: ability to collaborate closely with coworkers in a respectful and empathetic manner.
   
   



Benefits & Compensation

 - Annual Salary Target $135,000- $200,000 based on experience (Senior to Staff level)

 - Employee equity incentive plan

 - Health insurance: medical, vision, dental, ST & LT disability, and life

 - Gym membership stipend (up to $60/month)

 - Outdoor Recreation Reimbursement ($900 annual maximum)

 - Flexible hours (deliverable-based goals)

 - Flexible time off (just need manager approval)

 - Relocation package

 - Monthly company events

 - 401k program



The Company

H3X Technologies is a U.S.-based manufacturer of electric motors for sustainable aviation

source_jobs.title: Staff Power Electronics Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About the Role

At H3X https://www.h3x.tech/, we are building the most advanced electric motors in the world for sustainable aviation, marine, industrial, and defense applications. At 12 kW/kg continuous, the HPDM family of integrated motor drives is a step change in electric propulsion technology and removes one of the main barriers blocking widespread proliferation of electric and hybrid aircraft. Our motors range in size from 30 kW to 30 MW, include integrated drives, and are 3X lighter than competitors’ systems.

We are looking to hire a Senior or Staff Power Electronics Engineer who will oversee inverter development, taking the design from prototype to production for a next generation product line. The ideal candidate has experience in the entire product development cycle including requirements definition, design and analysis, and test and validation. You will be a key part of the core engineering team and will have a direct impact on the trajectory of the company.



Responsibilities

 - Own the power electronics hardware for high power density integrated motor drives, from requirements → concept → prototype → production readiness.

 - Translate product requirements into inverter flowdown requirements (performance, safety, reliability, manufacturability, testability).

 - Lead schematic design and high-density PCB layout (Altium), including stack-up, creepage/clearance strategy, high-current power paths, and gate-drive layout.

 - Design and optimize wide-bandgap power stages (SiC and/or GaN): switching behavior, gate drive, protection, sensing, isolation, and thermal/packaging constraints.

 - Engineer for low parasitics (power loop inductance, gate loop inductance, current sense fidelity) using simulation and measurement; apply tools like Ansys Q3D (or equivalent) where useful.

 - Develop and validate designs using circuit and system simulation (LTSpice / PLECS / MATLAB/Simulink or equivalent).

 - Drive EMI/EMC design and debug: grounding strategy, filtering (CMCs, Y-caps, etc.), shielding approaches, and layout practices; partner with test teams through pre-compliance and compliance efforts.

 - Build and execute hardware test and characterization plans (DVP&R): DPT, thermal, fault response, transient behavior (and more) across the operating envelope.

 - Be the “go-to” for debugging: efficiently isolate root cause across electrical/mechanical/firmware interfaces and implement corrective actions (design changes, manufacturing improvements, etc).

 - Partner with Supply Chain / Manufacturing on DFM/DFT, PCB fab/assembly coordination, inverter BOM ownership, and sustaining support through prototype builds.

 - Collaborate with motor control team to ensure clean interfaces (sensing, protection, comms, timing), and support integrated system bring-up in the lab.



Required Qualifications

 - 5+ years professional experience in power electronics hardware, with scope appropriate for Senior or Staff level ownership.

 - M.S./Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering or equivalent experience

 - Deep hands-on experience with high-density PCB layout for power electronics (Altium strongly preferred): disciplined component placement, current return paths, loop minimization, and mixed-signal best practices.

 - Demonstrated experience with wide-bandgap devices (SiC and/or GaN) and their practical design implications (gate drive, dv/dt, di/dt, protection, layout sensitivity), as well as high-voltage, high-current constraints: isolation strategy, creepage/clearance, partial discharge, etc.

 - Excellent debugging and bring-up skills: can take a new inverter from “first power” to stable operation; comfortable with oscilloscopes/differential probes, current probes, impedance considerations, and safe high-power lab practices.

 - Proficiency in at least two of: Altium, LTSpice/PLECS, MATLAB/Simulink, and comfort learning new tools quickly.

 - Strong fundamentals in power conversion, magnetics basics, sensing/isolation, protection, and contro

notes: High-tech manufacturing company with a strong focus on engineering roles.
